## Thread: MF 4.0 upgrade experience? — 3 msgs

### MF 4.0 upgrade experience?

- **From:** markd@bellsouth.net (MarkD)
- **Date:** 1998-10-16T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<98JV1.26167$lp3.2481539@news2.mia.bellsouth.net>`

```
I was tasked by my company to evaluate the impact of upgrading MF
COBOL 3.2 under AIX to version 4.0.  Has anyone done this upgrade yet
and/or have advice?  We have a large system with hundreds of of MF
COBOL programs run as .gnt files vs. compiled natively.  Between
development, production and Y2K testing, we simply can't afford any
gotchas while upgrading.

Thanks in advance
markd@bellsouth.net
```

#### ↳ Re: MF 4.0 upgrade experience?

- **From:** "Stephen D. Burns" <stephen.d.burns2@boeing.com>
- **Date:** 1998-10-16T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3627C322.71DF9F46@boeing.com>`
- **References:** `<98JV1.26167$lp3.2481539@news2.mia.bellsouth.net>`

```
If you use IMS and/or XDB you will have to upgrade those as well.  I
don't use the UNIX version, but the PC upgrade went real easy.  I had to
recompile all of my libraries.  The IMS people said I would not not need
to rebuild my IMS files, but I had to regen my PSB's and MFS files. 
Upgrade for Micro Focus get better every year....

MarkD wrote:
> 
> I was tasked by my company to evaluate the impact of upgrading MF
…[7 more quoted lines elided]…
> markd@bellsouth.net
```

#### ↳ Re: MF 4.0 upgrade experience?

- **From:** gcondon@bix.com (gcondon)
- **Date:** 1998-10-22T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<70og44$1hm@lotho.delphi.com>`
- **References:** `<98JV1.26167$lp3.2481539@news2.mia.bellsouth.net>`

```

: I was tasked by my company to evaluate the impact of upgrading MF
: COBOL 3.2 under AIX to version 4.0.  Has anyone done this upgrade yet
: and/or have advice?  We have a large system with hundreds of of MF

Skip 4.0 and go straight to 4.1.

Also, be sure to pull the latest patches from the
MF web site.

Greg
```

## Thread: MicroFocus Cobol Workbench v3.2x — 9 msgs

_Topics: [`Compilers and vendors`](topics.md#compilers)_

### MicroFocus Cobol Workbench v3.2x

- **From:** Bob Berman <bberman@netbox.com>
- **Date:** 1998-10-16T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3627E669.6B34@netbox.com>`

```
My client management was about to throw away a complete MF 3.2x
Workbench package, and I asked if I could have it, and they said yes ! 
There are about 2 dz manuals all in sealed boxes, and a package of disks
- at least 15, labeled Setup, Workbench, Toolkit, Cobol. I don't see any
gaps in the number sequence, but I've not had a chance to really
inventory them.  The package is labeled MF 3.2 for DOS/Windows/OS2.
There are also some early release OCX disks(??).

From what I could see, this seems to be a relatively current version, as
I see dates of 1994 in one of the manuals.  I think it is v3.21, but I
have not yet brought it home.

My problem is that there is so much there, I don't really know where to
start.  Can any one give me some guidance and direction ??

At the moment, I don't even have enough space on my hard drive for it -
time for a new one I guess.

Bob
```

#### ↳ Re: MicroFocus Cobol Workbench v3.2x

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 1998-10-16T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<708umo$9kh@dfw-ixnews5.ix.netcom.com>`
- **References:** `<3627E669.6B34@netbox.com>`

```

Bob Berman wrote in message <3627E669.6B34@netbox.com>...
>My client management was about to throw away a complete MF 3.2x
>Workbench package, and I asked if I could have it, and they said yes !
…[24 more quoted lines elided]…
>----+----+----+----+----+----+----+----+----+----+----+----+----+----+

Unless I am mistaken, you should be able to put in the 1st Workbench disk,
change to that drive and enter

     setup wb

If that doesn't work, do a
   dir *.ini

on the 1st Workbench disk and see what you get.

There are "minimal" installations that you can do, but tailoring such takes
some knowledge of MF.

I hopet this helps.
```

#### ↳ Re: MicroFocus Cobol Workbench v3.2x

- **From:** jets@nbnet.nb.ca (Tony M. Mina)
- **Date:** 1998-10-17T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<36291fe8.7826813@allnews.nb.sympatico.ca>`
- **References:** `<3627E669.6B34@netbox.com>`

```
The complete installation takes about 20Mb of disk space.  Version 3.2
is not really the latest. The last version for 3.2 is 3.2.5 and then
went up to v3.4.  Versions 3.2.5 and 3.4 are 16bit versions.

For the Workbench series you should have at least 17 manuals, 7 disks
for the Cobol, 5 disks for Toolset, 5 disks for Workbench, and 1disk
for Early Release.

To install, load the Workbench Disk 1 - Setup and run setup.exe then
follow the instructions on the screen. This will install workbench,
toolset and cobol.

If your version is below 3.2.5 you can download an upgrade to 3.2.5
from MF Website.

If you are using the product to develop software I would recommend you
look at Fujitsu Cobol v4.0 (www.adtools.com) as well as SP2 from
Flexus International (www.flexus.com).

If you need more info, email.

Good Luck.


Tony M. Mina
```

<a id="t-53a0ef233f"></a>
## Thread: Beginner Question — 6 msgs

_Topics: [`Tutorials, books, learning`](topics.md#learning)_

### Beginner Question

- **From:** "ABC" <stm@hongkong.com>
- **Date:** 1998-10-16T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<706b18$9bb$1@imsp009a.netvigator.com>`

```
Hello,

I just start writing COBOL for an assignment. The part I'm writing is to
process back order. I need to get a accounting date, and print the back
order.

I would like to ask, as said in the requirement, the accounting date may not
be the same as system date......this statement make me confuse, can someone
help me?
I just get the date and print the backorder for that date? Or print the
period?

Thanks!
```

#### ↳ Re: Beginner Question

- **From:** "Judson McClendon" <judmc123@bellsouth.net>
- **Date:** 1998-10-16T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<4qyV1.3112$9R2.2180628@news4.mia.bellsouth.net>`
- **References:** `<706b18$9bb$1@imsp009a.netvigator.com>`

```
ABC wrote:
>
>I just start writing COBOL for an assignment. The part I'm writing is to
…[7 more quoted lines elided]…
>period?

I've never heard the term 'accounting date', but in most business programs
you have a one, possibly two, dates that you may want to track.  For certain
you want the 'transaction date', which is the date the transaction actually
occurred.  This date will be input by the operator, because it may be earlier
than the date the transaction was entered into the computer.  Then you may
want to keep the 'entry date', which is the date the transaction was entered
into the computer, which can be automatically recorded by the program as the
transaction is entered, of course.  Hope this helps. :-)
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Beginner Question

- **From:** "Ted Pack" <tedpacknospam@thevision.net>
- **Date:** 1998-10-16T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<01bdf8b7$1c02efa0$7801d2d1@sranmojg>`
- **References:** `<706b18$9bb$1@imsp009a.netvigator.com> <4qyV1.3112$9R2.2180628@news4.mia.bellsouth.net>`

```
We use the term "accounting month ending date" and "accounting quarter
ending date" all the time. Our accountants pick a Friday to be the last day
of the month, quarter or year, since our people get paid on Friday and it
makes things neater.

You may get the "accounting date" from a file, from operator input, or by
calculation. (If it was ALWAYS the last Friday in the month you could
calculate it.)

Anyway, your first step is to find out where they keep the accounting date.

> ABC wrote:
I need to get a accounting date, and print the back

Judson McClendon wrote:
> I've never heard the term 'accounting date', . . .
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Beginner Question

- **From:** "Warren G. Simmons" <warrens@inficad.com>
- **Date:** 1998-10-16T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<36283B7B.37714464@inficad.com>`
- **References:** `<706b18$9bb$1@imsp009a.netvigator.com> <4qyV1.3112$9R2.2180628@news4.mia.bellsouth.net> <01bdf8b7$1c02efa0$7801d2d1@sranmojg>`

```
We often used the term monthly closing date. Reporting requirements
often demand that more needs to be done than could be done without
running the month on a logical basis vs. physical basis. A virtual
month. It is possible in some organizations to have many of these
because of the problems of consolidation, logistics...  A notice would
be published giving the various dates for those who needed to know.

Warren Simmons

Ted Pack wrote:
> 
> We use the term "accounting month ending date" and "accounting quarter
…[14 more quoted lines elided]…
> > I've never heard the term 'accounting date', . . .
```

## Thread: Fujitsu Cobol V4 Debugging? — 2 msgs

_Topics: [`Compilers and vendors`](topics.md#compilers)_

### Fujitsu Cobol V4 Debugging?

- **From:** "John T. Brasher" <johnbrasher@compuserve.com>
- **Date:** 1998-10-16T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<36279857.12B1F2F3@compuserve.com>`

```
I've installed 4.0 and can compile and link a program but when I try to
run it I get:
'Signal interrupt occured before reaching start of program'.

This is a program which is passed a linkage parameter.
How do I set it? (Execution-Time-Option?)
What is 'Start program'?

Each of these are in the 'Start Debugging' window.

AHWBA,

John T. Brasher FJ Cobol v4.0
```

#### ↳ Re: Fujitsu Cobol V4 Debugging?

- **From:** redsky@ibm.net (Thane Hubbell)
- **Date:** 1998-10-19T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<Jl0PnHJ5PvPd-pn2-PULnvvouyCb6@Dwight_Miller.iix.com>`
- **References:** `<36279857.12B1F2F3@compuserve.com>`

```
On Fri, 16 Oct 1998 19:02:47, "John T. Brasher" 
<johnbrasher@compuserve.com> wrote:

> I've installed 4.0 and can compile and link a program but when I try to
> run it I get:
…[7 more quoted lines elided]…
> 

Make sure the TEST compiler option is set, AND that you link with the 
DEBUG option.
```

<a id="t-fde5762754"></a>
## Thread: y2k rates / silicon valley / cobol — 3 msgs

_Topics: [`Y2K and Year-2000 remediation`](topics.md#y2k)_

### Re: y2k rates / silicon valley / cobol

- **From:** kiyoinc@ibm.XOUT.net (cory hamasaki)
- **Date:** 1998-10-16T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol,comp.software.year-2000
- **Message-ID:** `<7kepWhCNP4qd-pn2-UHLDehXtvtZQ@localhost>`
- **References:** `<706aok$ltd$1@nnrp1.dejanews.com>`

```
On Fri, 16 Oct 1998 02:24:20, dougr99@my-dejanews.com wrote:

> I am on a short term contract here in the Silicon Valley garnering $75
> hour W2 coding in an MVS environment.  It had been a while since I
…[7 more quoted lines elided]…
> 

Short answer is $75/hour W2 is fine.  Maybe a little low for a high cost
of living area but decent.   

The long answer is more interactive.

Is it just batch COBOL or does it include DB2, IMS, CICS, VSAM, etc?
Are you turning the crank on a remediation mill, looking for dates, 
ISPF'ing the source, and F3'ing or does the job include business logic 
analysis and an understanding of MVS as well.

I once spent a horrid two weeks figuring out how CA7 works, this was 
after attending a 3 day class on CA7.  What is it about mainframe 
software that the documention always answers questions that I don't 
have... the exceptions are the Principles of Operation, the PL/I 
Language Reference Manual, the Assember VS manual, and a couple others.

Turns out that CA7 is poorly documented.

Are you expected to have puzzled out these life mysteries?  It didn't 
sound like it in this case but sometimes it makes a difference if the
programmer's background is a match for the environment.   ...as always, 
it's better to have a sharp programmer than a dull acronym collector.

Is the rate W2 w/ benefits or w/o benefits.  Some people have benefits 
packages that are worth tens of thousands of dollars a year, or more.

If it's w/o benefits, that's still an OK rate, just not as good.

What do you mean by short term.  There's short term, long term, and 
perm.  I think of short term as 2 weeks to a month.  

I have a long term contract at slightly higher rates 1099.  If you know
the gig is going to last a few years, you can provide a break on the
rates.

For short term contract work, high intensity and with hard deadlines, 
the Dee Cee area is running well over $100/hour.   

I know dozens of people who are billing over $150/hour for long term...
well, I don't actually know the people, I know the guy who's paying
them... and the people don't get $150+/hour, they get a good salary and
their employer, the middleman, pockets the difference.

As always, getting these good rate gigs demands substantive
expertise and a demonstrated track record.  Keeping the gig 
requires performing the work, in spite of management, technology 
limitations, infrastructure failures, conflicting user requirements.

Are you paid overtime?  

Local conventions make a big difference in rates.  As far as mainframers
go, silicon valley was a depressed area until a year ago.  The guys in 
Fremont were always griping about MVS rates.  It looks like it's coming
back.   This summer, they dialed their rates to $200/hour but that's 
short term and very high intensity.

It's hard to project out more than a few months.   Are we in a spiral
into a depression?  Will Y2K trigger a massive rebuilding of the IT 
infrastructure, with ever increasing rates for real programmers.

I don't know; this is one of those things that could go either way.

There's another article above... about Motorcycles...

--------------Quote-----------------------
A tiny bit of research will turn up all sorts of doomsayers, but little
real "information". I didn't read the wired article in question, and
possibly there are real concerns. What I *do* know is that all the crap
I've read about the problem that relates to anything about which I have
my own first hand objective knowledge is crap. The same is true with
everyone else I know who works in hardware/software industry; they've
mostly already fixed the critical (ie the systems will just lay down
and die) problems *if there were any* and will have most of the time
wasting/feature reducing bugs fixed long before the end of next year.
 
Or as one guy put it, the unix guys are sure they are okay, but fear
the mainframes. The mainframe guys are sure they are okay, but fear the
embedded systems. The embedded systems guys seem to think its no big
deal, but fear ... etc. etc. etc.
---------------End Quote----------------------
Not correct for the Dee Cee arena...  Here, there's an incredible 
reality disconnect.   If you ask the Y2K Project Office, you get the "on
schedule for completion by December 1998, leaving all of 1999 for 
testing." line.  If you ask the code-head in the trenches, he'll dart 
his eyes around and tell you that they haven't started yet... 

what! but they're telling us that System-X is tested and in production..

not started yet,  Oh, someone did a study about a year ago and we've 
identified a technical approach but no one has touched a line of code.

It may not be fair to extrapolate from my specific experiences in Dee 
Cee to an indictment of hornhaired management around the country but
that's the story from the potomac.

Some companies are close to completion by December 1998 but this is not
the general rule.   Several firms have reported substantive progress to
WDC Y2K and their IT people have spoken on the effort, the cost and
commitment.

Unfortunately, reps from other firms report that they are not in good
shape.

Note to -bks-, you're not the one who's supposed to hand out the maps 
to the food storage and manufacturing plants.  It's the ones who are 
worried about the starving masses and the rioting bureaucrats.   When
"they" come for your 14 month old dried beans and canned eel, just hand 
out the maps and shout out, "Here's where the food is, let's get it,
who's with me?!!" 

...as the mob rushes for the Conagra or ADM facility, you slip away...
if anyone notices, just say, "I'm fainting from hunger, save some for me
boys....."

cory hamasaki 441 days, 10,601 hours.

```

```
>
```

## Thread: IBM COBOL under LE - Performance Info — 2 msgs

_Topics: [`Compilers and vendors`](topics.md#compilers)_

### IBM COBOL under LE - Performance Info

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 1998-10-16T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<708aqd$ag6@sjx-ixn5.ix.netcom.com>`

```
In another thread, I was asked about "dynamic CALL" performance
considerations.  In response to this question, I was pointed to an EXCELLENT
presentation (from a recent GUIDE/SHARE) meeting.  I would suggest that
anyone who is interested in performance issues for COBOL under LE (and has a
PDF reader) look at:

   http://www1.s390.ibm.com/le/conference/s6214tr.pdf
```

```

> 
(snip)
>    http://www1.s390.ibm.com/le/conference/s6214tr.pdf

Bill Lynch
```

#### ↳ Re: Rmcobol aplication /oracle

- **From:** Albert Ratzlaff <albert@infonet.com.py>
- **Date:** 1998-10-17T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3628C29E.AE5B8C73@infonet.com.py>`
- **References:** `<362764B3.F97B7CBC@atm.mailcom.pt>`

```


Conceiï¿½ï¿½o Sousa wrote:

> I have a aplication developped with rmcobol85 version 6.09 and i would
> like to know if it is possible to acess to a BD oracle 7.3.2 with my
…[7 more quoted lines elided]…
>

You can use DB/Plus, or whatever it's name is now. Absolute transparent access
to Oracle without recompiling. The only problem is, no transactions. Every
WRITE, REWRITE, DELETE are commited inmediatly. Look at http://www.liant.com.

Regards
Albert Ratzlaff
```

```
 76,000 people in plants and offices all over the world.  They
encourage you to learn different skills and rely heavily on promoting

its best.  This member will provide technical application expertise
contributing to the support of those applications that focus on the
order cycle of their business.  This is a great opportunity to learn
new skills!

 in a diverse team environment, Application and customer support
experience, Excellent problem solving skills, Proficient at quickly
comprehending new applications, Mainframe CICS skills, VSAM
experience, Willingness to retrain in PC/Unix platform skills, DB2
experience is a plus!

short and long term disability, tuition reimbursement, 401(k), pension
plan, stock option plan, 10 paid vacation days, 11 paid holidays and
more!
```

```
Are there other users of Fujitsu Cobol (v4.0) having problems in
installing the runtimes?

I tried to install the runtimes for Power Cobol and Power Form and I
got an error saying that it cannot locate _setup.dll and _isres.dll.
The _setup.dll is on the directory but the _isres.dll is not.  What I
did was copy all the files from the CD (...tools\patch\rts....) to our
network drive and run the setup from the network and it gave me this
error.  So what I did was copy again the sames files from the CD to my
local drive and got the same error.  But if I run the setup directly
from the CD it works fine.

The runtime for Cobol 97 works fine on the network.  No errors at all.

Any help will be greatly appreciated.  Thanks in advance.


Tony M. Mina
jets@nbnet.nb.ca
```

### OT - humor

- **From:** "Judson McClendon" <judmc123@bellsouth.net>
- **Date:** 1998-10-17T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** alt.cobol,alt.lang.basic,comp.lang.basic.misc,comp.lang.cobol,comp.programming
- **Message-ID:** `<lH4W1.7406$r%6.3056858@news3.mia.bellsouth.net>`

```
I found this sign in a mainframe computer room years ago.
(With apologies to our German friends. :-)


                        ACHTUNG!
                        --------
Das machine is nicht fur gerfingerpoken und mittengrabben.
   Ist easy schnappen der Sprinngwerk, blowenfusen und
             poppencorken mit spitzensparken.
Ist nicht fur gewerken by das Dummkopfen.  Das rubbernecken
         sightseeren keepen hands in das Pockets.
          Relaxen und watch das blinkenlights...
```

#### ↳ Re: OT - humor

- **From:** "William J. Leary Jr." <Bill_Leary@msn.com>
- **Date:** 1998-10-17T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** alt.cobol,alt.lang.basic,comp.lang.basic.misc,comp.lang.cobol,comp.programming
- **Message-ID:** `<OleawNj#9GA.212@upnetnews03>`
- **References:** `<lH4W1.7406$r%6.3056858@news3.mia.bellsouth.net>`

```

Judson McClendon wrote in message ...
>I found this sign in a mainframe computer room years ago.
>          Relaxen und watch das blinkenlights...


Ah, yes, the classic "blinkenlights"...

It's so classic, it even got into the Jargon file
 http://www.fwi.uva.nl/~mes/jargon/ ).

blinkenlights /blink'n-litz/ /n./ Front-panel diagnostic lights on a
computer, esp. a dinosaur. Derives from the last word of the famous
blackletter-Gothic sign in mangled pseudo-German that once graced about half
the computer rooms in the English-speaking world.

    - Bill
```

- **Date:** 1998-10-17T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** alt.cobol,alt.lang.basic,comp.lang.basic.misc,comp.lang.cobol,comp.programming

```
Here . . . this is even more on topic:


DECLARE SUB GOOFENHAHA

CLS

GOOFENHAHA

END


SUB GOOFENHAHA()

    PRINT "                         ACHTUNG!"
    PRINT "                         --------"
    PRINT "Das machine is nicht fur gerfingerpoken und mittengrabben."
    PRINT "Ist easy schnappen der Sprinngwerk, blowenfusen und"
    PRINT "              poppencorken mit spitzensparken."
    PRINT "Ist nicht fur gewerken by das Dummkopfen.  Das
rubbernecken"
    PRINT "          sightseeren keepen hands in das Pockets."
    PRINT "           Relaxen und watch das blinkenlights..."


RETURN

There.  Are you happy?

jdm
```

<a id="t-0c4a243cb2"></a>
## Thread: porting AS400-COBOL applications to PC-network — 5 msgs

_Topics: [`Migration and conversion`](topics.md#migration) · [`AS/400, iSeries, RPG`](topics.md#as400)_

### porting AS400-COBOL applications to PC-network

- **From:** "Paul Mortelmans" <info@atait.com>
- **Date:** 1998-10-17T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<70a3d5$4nt$1@xenon.inbe.net>`

```
Hello,

I hope to be at the right place and that someone can advice me
.
Our boss has decided that our AS400 has to leave the company end 99. He
wants me to take the COBOL applications and 'copy' them on a PC-network.

We have developed these applications ourselves a few yeaers ago  and are
very happy with the result.

I am looking for a 'porting' or 'rehosting' or 'conversion' tool, allowing
me to convert our sources to code accepted by the PC-network platform. I am
also looking for the right database system for the converted data.

Has someone experience with this kind of task ? Can I still use COBOL ? Can
I add some GUI-effects ????

Thanks

Paul Mortelmans
```

#### ↳ Re: porting AS400-COBOL applications to PC-network

- **From:** redsky@ibm.net (Thane Hubbell)
- **Date:** 1998-10-19T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<Jl0PnHJ5PvPd-pn2-52fiGBh796WF@Dwight_Miller.iix.com>`
- **References:** `<70a3d5$4nt$1@xenon.inbe.net>`

```
On Sat, 17 Oct 1998 12:35:35, "Paul Mortelmans" <info@atait.com> 
wrote:

> Hello,
> 
…[7 more quoted lines elided]…
> 

Did you use SDA?

> I am looking for a 'porting' or 'rehosting' or 'conversion' tool, allowing
> me to convert our sources to code accepted by the PC-network platform. I am
> also looking for the right database system for the converted data.
> 

Did you utilize the relational database functions of the AS/400?

> Has someone experience with this kind of task ? Can I still use COBOL ? Can
> I add some GUI-effects ????
> 

Since you PROBABLY used SDA, AND the datbase of the AS/400 - I suggest
converting to Oracle, using Fujitsu COBOL, and COBOL sp2 for the Gui.

http://www.oracle.com
http://www.adtools.com
http://www.flexus.com

Oracle - Premier Reliational database
Fujitsu - COBOL, Windows, network optimized, works well with Oracle
COBOL sp2 - the interface of SP2 would be EASY to convert to from SDA 
and will give you a GUI.
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: porting AS400-COBOL applications to PC-network

- **From:** rtwolfe@flexus.com (Bob Wolfe)
- **Date:** 1998-10-19T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<362ba58a.29045057@news.enter.net>`
- **References:** `<70a3d5$4nt$1@xenon.inbe.net> <Jl0PnHJ5PvPd-pn2-52fiGBh796WF@Dwight_Miller.iix.com>`

```
redsky@ibm.net (Thane Hubbell) wrote:

>On Sat, 17 Oct 1998 12:35:35, "Paul Mortelmans" <info@atait.com> 
>wrote:
…[36 more quoted lines elided]…
>

WARNING!  The shameless promotion you are about to read is true...only
the names have been changed to protect the innocent!

Paul...

Thane is absolutely correct.  The SDA screens would be pretty easy to
convert over to sp2 by creating an automated conversion tool.  We've
created a number of conversion tools with sp2, but not one for SDA.

Because sp2 provides you with the ability to create GUI screen
definitions dynamically from a COBOL program, it's pretty easy to
create screen converters.

I'll be happy to take this discussion off line so as not to offend my
pals in the comp.lang.cobol newsgroup with my blatant commercialism!

My e-mail address is rtwolfe@flexus.com.


Bob Wolfe, flexus
Check out The Flexus COBOL Page at http://www.flexus.com
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: porting AS400-COBOL applications to PC-network

- **From:** gvwmoore@ix.netcom.com (G Moore)
- **Date:** 1998-10-20T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<36370a85.10291297@nntp.ix.netcom.com>`
- **References:** `<70a3d5$4nt$1@xenon.inbe.net> <Jl0PnHJ5PvPd-pn2-52fiGBh796WF@Dwight_Miller.iix.com>`

```
paul mortelamans wrote:

>> I am looking for a 'porting' or 'rehosting' or 'conversion' tool, allowing
>> me to convert our sources to code accepted by the PC-network platform. I am
>> also looking for the right database system for the converted data.

well, before you convert the code, you have other issues:
a> is all the code in 1 language?
b> is that code in 1 language written for porting (no comp-5 fields)?
c>are you attempting to port it all, and if not, which parts are you
going to choose to port over another, and more importantly why?

if i were doing a porting project, i would port the parts which are
not needed 24/7, make sure they have UPS, mirror their drive to
another machine, ect. however, since you are talking about databases,
there are 2 questions:

a> are you porting for PC administrators who want as400 access? or
b>are you moving applications off the mainframe for local businesses
who want to maintain it themselves?



gvwmoore@ix.spam.netcom.com to reply remove the spam
```

#### ↳ Re: porting AS400-COBOL applications to PC-network

- **From:** john@smithjd.demon.co.uk
- **Date:** 1998-10-21T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<362e4a4e.7874492@news.demon.co.uk>`
- **References:** `<70a3d5$4nt$1@xenon.inbe.net>`

```
I will applogise in advance for anyone I upset but is your boss really
sure he wants to do this. From my experience the AS/400 is way above
any PC operating system in trerms of reliability, security etc etc. I
would suggest moving to a pc platform is a retro step. If you want to
add gui what about using Graphical Access you get alot of the effect
for nill work alternativley there are many quality products to help.

Do you really want to move your data from the most popular data base
in the world ?

JD
```

```
the value to a pic size like so:

variable: N=3


DOB-In                    pic x(N).

dat file.

any help would be appreciated.
```

#### ↳ Re: getting input from the keyboard....

- **From:** vbandke@ibm.net (Volker Bandke)
- **Date:** 1998-10-17T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3628ea60.4784585@news3.ibm.net>`
- **References:** `<19981017061744.05263.00001480@ng-fc1.aol.com>`

```
On 17 Oct 1998 10:17:44 GMT, savggerbil@aol.com (SavgGerbil) wrote:

>hi, can someone tell me the command(s) used to get/read input from the keyboard
>and store them in a variable? 

Yes, I can <g> . Use the ACCEPT statement (see Manual)

>also, if the variable is numeric, could i move
>the value to a pic size like so:
…[4 more quoted lines elided]…
>DOB-In                    pic x(N).
You want a variable picture string.  This is not directly possible.  But you could do
something like this:
01 N PIC S9(4) Binary.
01 DOB-IN.
     05 DOB-In-Chars  PIX X Occurs 1 to 4096 TIMES depending on N.

>
>
>i want to be able to ask for a number and then move that number of spaces in a
>dat file.

In the procedure devision:

	ACCEPT N ......
	MOVE DOB-IN to WHEREVER-YOU-WANT

>
>any help would be appreciated.

HTH

with kind regards

Volker Bandke
(BSP GmbH)
```

#### ↳ Re: getting input from the keyboard....

- **From:** "J���rg Laarmann" <J.Laarmann@online.de>
- **Date:** 1998-10-18T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<362A4C05.8F03E39C@online.de>`
- **References:** `<19981017061744.05263.00001480@ng-fc1.aol.com>`

```


SavgGerbil schrieb:

> hi, can someone tell me the command(s) used to get/read input from the keyboard
> and store them in a variable?

Use ACCEPT. It would be helpful if you tell which OS you use.

> also, if the variable is numeric, could i move
> the value to a pic size like so:
…[4 more quoted lines elided]…
>

You can't do that this way. I think you want to use an variable length PIC X
field.You have to use a structre for defining DOB-In. First a two bytes
length-field, then the storage for the variable length item. It's import to use 49
as level number. Refer your manual for details.

Have a nice day.
Joerg

> i want to be able to ask for a number and then move that number of spaces in a
> dat file.
>
> any help would be appreciated.
```

## Thread: How to display a text in color on the screen?? — 2 msgs

_Topics: [`Help requests and how-to`](topics.md#help)_

### How to display a text in color on the screen??

- **From:** "sylvain duval" <sylvain.duval@village.uunet.be>
- **Date:** 1998-10-17T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<70b0ku$sj4$1@xenon.inbe.net>`

```
Is there a special command to display a message in color??
thank you to answer my question!
```

#### ↳ Re: How to display a text in color on the screen??

- **From:** "Donald Tees" <donald@willmack.com>
- **Date:** 1998-10-17T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<70b5ft$9va$1@news.igs.net>`
- **References:** `<70b0ku$sj4$1@xenon.inbe.net>`

```

sylvain duval wrote in message <70b0ku$sj4$1@xenon.inbe.net>...
>Is there a special command to display a message in color??
>thank you to answer my question!


It tends to be compiler dependent, but look under the display
verb. Something along the line of:

        Display "this is in light blue on dark blue"
                with background-color 1
                       foreground-color 3.
```

<a id="t-d1297f2e57"></a>
## Thread: Fujitsu COBOL85, V3 question - Help please! — 5 msgs

_Topics: [`Compilers and vendors`](topics.md#compilers) · [`COBOL standards (ANS, ISO, 74/85/2002/2014)`](topics.md#standards)_

### Fujitsu COBOL85, V3 question - Help please!

- **From:** "Kira S. Anastasia" <kirasa@oz.net>
- **Date:** 1998-10-17T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<36294C14.A812CF2@oz.net>`

```
Hi Folks,

  Thanks in advanced.

  Back into COBOL after a long absence :-).

  I picked up the Fujitsu Compiler and have been working through some of
the examples.  Docs are a little rough for this day and age, but
reminiscent of my days in the "System Library" :-).

 The problem I ran into is with sample4.cob.  I finally figured out the
method of file creation for it and have that in place.  When I execute
the program (sample4.exe), I get the following message:

Jmp0311I-U Missing Allocation.  File=Outfile. Pgm=sample4 adr 00411EC.

I understand what is happening, but I don't know how to fix it.  The
file is specified as follows in the example code:

-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-= Extracted from sample4.cob
-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-
000100* Kira's Second shot screen handling...
000110 IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
000200  PROGRAM-ID. SAMPLE4.
000300*
000400 ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.
000500  CONFIGURATION SECTION.
000600  SPECIAL-NAMES.
000700      CRT STATUS  IS  SCREEN-STATUS.
000800  INPUT-OUTPUT SECTION.
000900  FILE-CONTROL.
001000      SELECT    INDEXED-FILE
001100                ASSIGN       TO  OUTFILE
001200                ORGANIZATION IS  INDEXED
001300                RECORD KEY   IS  ID-NUMBER
001400                ALTERNATE RECORD KEY IS NAME-VALUE
001500                ACCESS MODE  IS  RANDOM.
001600*
[ ... ]
DATA DIVISION.
001800  FILE SECTION.
001900  FD   INDEXED-FILE.
002000  01   INDEXED-RECORD.
002100       02  ID-NUMBER     PIC 9(6).
002200       02  NAME-VALUE    PIC X(40).
[...]
004500 PROCEDURE DIVISION.
004600*
004700      OPEN OUTPUT INDEXED-FILE.
[...]
-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=- End enclosed sample code -=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-

Unless my memory is faultier than I think (too many years of Jolt,
twinkies and vending machine pizza at odd hours ;-), the file name
should be "INDEXED-FILE".

This has been created and is present in the
c:\fsc\pcobol32\samples\sample4 directory along with the executable
(sample4.exe).  Since the program is running from that directory, I
would think it should find it.

I've found nothing in the help files on this, dunno if it is missing,
assumed or if I just missed it (latter likely?!). If such is not the
case, should I make the following modification (as I recalled this
worked in the last version of RM COBOL I used :-):

001000      SELECT    "C:\fsc\pcobol32\samples\sample4\INDEXED-FILE"

Any help, thoughts, directions or pointers much appreciated.

Thanks & take care, Kira.
```

#### ↳ Re: Fujitsu COBOL85, V3 question - Help please!

- **From:** rkrayhawk@aol.com (RKRayhawk)
- **Date:** 1998-10-18T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<19981017222158.02586.00001987@ng01.aol.com>`
- **References:** `<36294C14.A812CF2@oz.net>`

```

Probably should not try this, but just for fun.

I have not used Fujitsu COBOL85, V3 (or any other Fujitsu product), but should
not the ASSIGN clause determine the external name?  Won't the program be
looking for somethings like 

C:\fsc\pcobol32\samples\sample4\OUTFILE"

I would think that "INDEXED-FILE" is strictly the internal name, visible only
to the program source code (and gone after compilation, other than residual
information in debug areas).

The external name is noted in your error message, is it not?
 Jmp0311I-U Missing Allocation.  File=Outfile. Pgm=sample4 adr 00411EC.

Think about this, if your coded your own proposed solution , to wit,

001000      SELECT    "C:\fsc\pcobol32\samples\sample4\INDEXED-FILE"

How would you express the OPEN/CLOSE statements.

perhaps some thing like

001000      SELECT    INDEXED-FILE
001100                ASSIGN       TO 
"C:\fsc\pcobol32\samples\sample4\INDEXED-FILE" 
etc. etc. 

is a viable syntax with your Fujitsu tool.

All of this is guess work, hope it helps. Also it stands to reason that there
would be default file extensions that differ for sequential files and index
files.


Best WIshes.
,



Robert Rayhawk
RKRayhawk@aol.com
```

#### ↳ Re: Fujitsu COBOL85, V3 question - Help please!

- **From:** sff5ky@aol.com (Sff5ky)
- **Date:** 1998-10-18T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<19981017221848.24717.00000852@ngol07.aol.com>`
- **References:** `<36294C14.A812CF2@oz.net>`

```

Being as you are opening the file output, you may be having a problem with the
fact that a file already exists with the assigned file name.
 BTW, what name was applied to OUTFILE in the runtime environment ?
Execution of the program brings up a runtime environment dialog where you would
SET OUTFILE=<some file name>
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Fujitsu COBOL85, V3 question - Help please!

- **From:** "Kira S. Anastasia" <kirasa@oz.net>
- **Date:** 1998-10-17T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<36297B60.5E4E27A1@oz.net>`
- **References:** `<36294C14.A812CF2@oz.net> <19981017221848.24717.00000852@ngol07.aol.com>`

```
Sff5ky wrote:
> 
> Being as you are opening the file output, you may be having a problem with the
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
> SET OUTFILE=<some file name>

This was the key to resolving this delemma!

After some rooting around, I did find the answer to this <blush>, of
course, after I posted the previous message :-/.  The answer is located
at:

http://www.adtools.com/support/support.htm

Deleted the file I went through so much to create (educational in any
case :-), set the environment variable and off we went!

Thanks much to everyone who responded!

Take care, Kira.
```

#### ↳ Re: Fujitsu COBOL85, V3 question - Help please!

- **From:** "Donald Tees" <donald@willmack.com>
- **Date:** 1998-10-18T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<70ch70$445$1@news.igs.net>`
- **References:** `<36294C14.A812CF2@oz.net>`

```

Kira S. Anastasia wrote in message <36294C14.A812CF2@oz.net>...
>
>Jmp0311I-U Missing Allocation.  File=Outfile. Pgm=sample4 adr 00411EC.


A missing allocation, Fujitsu speak, is a missing disk area or drive.
I have noticed some of the samples were set up for drive D.  That
may be the problem.
```

<a id="t-9ce3b98baf"></a>
## Thread: Win95 API Question — 3 msgs

### Win95 API Question

- **From:** bob7536@aol.com (Bob7536)
- **Date:** 1998-10-17T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<19981016205338.17670.00001784@ng120.aol.com>`

```
Hello,

I am looking for a Win95 API that will allow me to change the "Task Bar
Options" / "Always on Top" selection.  I have looked in the Win32 Software
Development Kit that comes with NetExpress, but i have not been able to locate
the proper API to allow me to modify this selection.  Is anyone aware of the
name of this API ?

Thanks in advance,
Bob Hennessey
```

#### ↳ Re: Win95 API Question

- **From:** "Larry Howe" <larry@comjet.com>
- **Date:** 1998-10-18T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<ckfW1.313$fl.4120007@audrey2.cais.com>`
- **References:** `<19981016205338.17670.00001784@ng120.aol.com>`

```
Bob,

I found where you can retrieve the always on top state. However I could not
find how to set it. HTH.

Larry


SHAppBarMessage


WINSHELLAPI UINT APIENTRY SHAppBarMessage(
    DWORD dwMessage,
    PAPPBARDATA pData
);

Sends an appbar message to the system.

Returns a message-dependent value. For more information, see the Microsoft
Platform SDK documentation for the appbar message sent.
dwMessage
Appbar message value to send. This parameter can be one of the following
values: ABM_ACTIVATE  Notifies the system that an appbar has been activated.
ABM_GETAUTOHIDEBAR  Retrieves the handle to the autohide appbar associated
with a particular edge of the screen.
ABM_GETSTATE  Retrieves the autohide and always-on-top states of the Windows
taskbar.
ABM_GETTASKBARPOS  Retrieves the bounding rectangle of the Windows taskbar.
ABM_NEW  Registers a new appbar and specifies the message identifier that
the system should use to send notification messages to the appbar.
ABM_QUERYPOS  Requests a size and screen position for an appbar.
ABM_REMOVE  Unregisters an appbar, removing the bar from the system's
internal list.
ABM_SETAUTOHIDEBAR  Registers or unregisters an autohide appbar for an edge
of the screen.
ABM_SETPOS  Sets the size and screen position of an appbar.
ABM_WINDOWPOSCHANGED  Notifies the system when an appbar's position has
changed.

pData
Address of an APPBARDATA structure. The content of the structure depends on
the value set in the dwMessage parameter.




Bob7536 wrote in message <19981016205338.17670.00001784@ng120.aol.com>...
>Hello,
>
>I am looking for a Win95 API that will allow me to change the "Task Bar
>Options" / "Always on Top" selection.  I have looked in the Win32 Software
>Development Kit that comes with NetExpress, but i have not been able to
locate
>the proper API to allow me to modify this selection.  Is anyone aware of
the
>name of this API ?
>
>Thanks in advance,
>Bob Hennessey
```

- **Date:** 1998-10-18T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<19981018082113.24661.00002292@ng119.aol.com>`
- **References:** `<ckfW1.313$fl.4120007@audrey2.cais.com>`

## Thread: new cobol book available — 1 msgs

_Topics: [`Tutorials, books, learning`](topics.md#learning)_

### new cobol book available

- **From:** d.s.kirk@ix.netcom.com (David)
- **Date:** 1998-10-17T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<362af953.12275444@nntp.ix.netcom.com>`

```
A couple of months ago i posted that this book would soon be
available. well, now amazon.com is showing it. there were some delays
at printer.   

title:  "COBOL for OS/390 Power Programming"	
audience: experienced cobol programmers. the book does NOT teach
syntax or basic commands. the book assumes the reader knows cobol. the
focus of the book is on power techniques to help the reader master the
language environment, not just a few verbs or techniques.  
environment: MVS with IMS or CICS or DB2 or combinations. each
particular environment is addressed
where to find:  www.mvs-training.com    or   www.amazon.com    
featured: all Y2K commands for cobol are included, including MLE.
there is a special section on a Y2K supplier and even info on IBM's
late summer announcement about object-code Y2K remediation.   
thanks for listening...  I don't mean to intrude. 
David
```

```
out there called Host Print.  Just execute your program and then open the
output file you created in Host Print.  It will print in landscape mode.
Just go to www.download.com and do a search for "Host Print Utililty".  Then
download "Host Print Utility 1.1".

Josh Larson
------------------
joshlarson@home.com
```

```
Technical Summary
Proven experience of over 4 years on IBM Mainframe in coding and testing
of application software on IBM/3090, ES/9000 under MVS/ESA and used
tools like CICS, DB2, VSAM & JCL at a large Integrated Public Sector
Plant.


Qualification    BS Degree

Technical Skills
Hardware    IBM ES/9000
RDBMS    DB2 3.0, Oracle 7.0
Languages     COBOL, C, JCL, Pascal, FORTRAN
GUI     Power Builder, Windows
O/S     MVS/ESA, UNIX, DOS
Tools     Xpediter, CEDF, FILE-AID
Specialization    DB2, CICS, VS-COBOL II, JCL, MVS


Major Projects
Project    Year   2000  project   May�98-Tilldate
Client     Palm Beach  County
Hardware    IBM, ES9000
Software    VSAM, VS-COBOL,CICS,DB2

Description

The  objective of this project was to convert programs and files
identified by  the client  for year 2000 compliance. This is achieved by
expanding the year fields to add  the century portions by using the
windowing technique (sliding window  and fixed  window  technique).

Responsibilities:
Analyzed programs.
Generated control file.
Generated report of impacted variables.
Expanded  the year fields to add the century portions.
Performed testing of new codes.








Project  State Wide Automated Welfare System            Aug �97- April
�98
Client   Health Welfare Data Center, Sacramento, CA.
Hardware  IBM, ES9000
Software  VS-COBOL II, DB2, CICS
Tools    Xpediter, CEDF, File-AID

Description

HWDC(Health Welfare Data Center) was entrusted with the job of
developing a State Wide Automated Welfare System.  The main objective of
this is

1. Electronic Exchange of data between consortia system for eligibility
Antifraud and Case Management.
2. Establishing interface requirements for all SAWS Consortia Welfare
System and other related State Wide Automated Welfare System
3. Partnering with California Department of Social Services,  Department
of Health Services and consortia to define specification to meet state
and federal reporting.




Project             Human Resources Information System
Dec�96-July�97
Client   Visakhapatnam Steel Plant
Hardware  IBM-3090
Software  VS-COBOL II, CICS, DB2


Description
HIRS is an Online System which helps the Personnel Department to manage
the Human Resources of the company.  The Company has over 10,000
permanent employees and has over 12,000 temporary workers.  This system
maintains information about all employee�s working in Visakhapatnam
Steel Plant.  It has the following major modules.

-Employee Profile Maintenance
-Personnel Services Maintenance
The Employee Profile Maintenance module maintains all professional and
personal information about each employee.  The module developed has over
100 programs.

Role
Member of a 6 member development team for coding, testing and
maintaining the employee profile module.  Has written over 20 programs
for data entry.  Developed programs for online query and report
generation.





Project              Marketing Management System         Aug�96-Nov�96
Organization   Visakhapatnam Steel Plant
Hardware  IBM-3090
Software  VS-COBOL II, CICS, DB2

Description
The on-line application product which runs under CICS by invoking
TransID HOMS has many subsystems.  I was involved in the development of
Preparation of Stock Transfer Chalan module.  Stock transfer Challans
are prepared for dispatch to various branches.  Preparation of stock
challan provides the following options

? Creation of STC for Rail dispatches
? Creation of STC for Road dispatches
? Display of STC
? Reprinting of STC

Role
As one among the three member team was responsible for developing the
STC module.  Has developed various data entry and validation programs
for STC creation.  Developed batch programs for printing STC.




Project  On-line Work Order System(OWOS) Apr.�95-Jul�96.
Organization  Visakhapatnam Steel Plant

Description
This OLTP based system helps in maintaining up to-date status of all
```

<a id="t-fe88b13637"></a>
## Thread: Calculating on 20 digits ammount? — 11 msgs

### Calculating on 20 digits ammount?

- **From:** Einar Clementz <eclementz@bigfoot.com>
- **Date:** 1998-10-19T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<362B44D8.24DB2691@bigfoot.com>`

```

Hi out there.

COBOL has a limit of 18 digits in picture 9 fields. This is not enough
for a solution we are working on. We need 20 digits amount fields. The
current version use 18 digits, 16 integers and 2 decimals. We looking
for a solution where we can adapt to 20 digits without to much recoding.

Is there anyone who have algorithm for such a situation?


Thanks a lot.

Regards Einar Clementz.
```

#### ↳ Re: Calculating on 20 digits ammount?

- **From:** "Judson McClendon" <judmc123@bellsouth.net>
- **Date:** 1998-10-19T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<k4HW1.48$Nk3.51173@news2.mia.bellsouth.net>`
- **References:** `<362B44D8.24DB2691@bigfoot.com>`

```
Einar Clementz wrote:
>
>COBOL has a limit of 18 digits in picture 9 fields. This is not enough
…[4 more quoted lines elided]…
>Is there anyone who have algorithm for such a situation?

The latest version of the proposed new COBOL standard specifies a 31
digit limit, instead of 18.  If you can find a compiler which supports
that, you should be in business.

Failing that, it sounds like you need some extended precision functions.
Algorithms for multi-precision arithmetic abound, and are almost trivial
for add and subtract.  Multiply is not too much harder, but divide can
be a bit more complex to do well.  If you want to see some very high
precision (> 1000 digits) decimal floating point code in C, including
transcendentals like sqrt, Y^X, trig and logs, download BIGCALC.ZIP from
my web page below.  BIGCALC is a high precision RPN calculator program.
The math routines are pretty solid and have been licensed for use in two
commercial applications from other companies. The code is well commented,
especially the math routines.  The routines may be massive overkill for
your needs, but the comments should be helpful if you decide to code the
functions yourself.
```

#### ↳ Re: Calculating on 20 digits ammount?

- **From:** "Tom Morrison" <t.morrison@liant.com>
- **Date:** 1998-10-19T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<70fl5s$2r1$1@client2.news.psi.net>`
- **References:** `<362B44D8.24DB2691@bigfoot.com>`

```
Einar Clementz wrote in message <362B44D8.24DB2691@bigfoot.com>...
>COBOL has a limit of 18 digits in picture 9 fields. This is not enough
>for a solution we are working on. We need 20 digits amount fields. The
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
>Is there anyone who have algorithm for such a situation?


You did not specify your system/compiler.  If you have some flexibility in
this choice, RM/COBOL supports 30 digits.  See www.liant.com.

---
Tom Morrison
Liant Software Corporation
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Calculating on 20 digits ammount?

- **From:** "Leif Svalgaard" <leif@ibm.net>
- **Date:** 1998-10-19T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<362b9f56.0@news3.ibm.net>`
- **References:** `<362B44D8.24DB2691@bigfoot.com> <70fl5s$2r1$1@client2.news.psi.net> <362BAB40.259FF19C@online.no>`

```
The ARITPK package (part of ETKPAK at  http://www.etk.com/download) allows
you to work with numbers with the implied picture PIC S((18)V9(18).
ARITPK will compile in any Cobol environment, even old ones.
If you have a newer compiler, you wouldn't be asking.

in

>
```

#### ↳ Re: Calculating on 20 digits ammount?

- **From:** WOB@my-dejanews.com
- **Date:** 1998-10-20T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<70h8c9$gl2$1@nnrp1.dejanews.com>`
- **References:** `<362B44D8.24DB2691@bigfoot.com>`

```
In article <362B44D8.24DB2691@bigfoot.com>,
  Einar Clementz <eclementz@bigfoot.com> wrote:
>
> Hi out there.
…[11 more quoted lines elided]…
>

Einar,

Assuming you are using an IBM-Mainframe platform, one alternative would be to
write a small ASSEMBLER program that can be called from COBOL. ASSEMBLER can
utilize up to 16-Bytes (31 Digits) in Packed-Decimal format. The subprogram
can then perform the aithmetic needed by your system. You can preserve your
existing definitions using this method.

Contact me privately if you'd like an example.

HTH....

Cheers,

WOB

-----------== Posted via Deja News, The Discussion Network ==----------
http://www.dejanews.com/       Search, Read, Discuss, or Start Your Own
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Calculating on 20 digits ammount?

- **From:** Howard Brazee <NOSPAMbrazee@home.com>
- **Date:** 1998-10-20T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<362C9731.3F19E68B@home.com>`
- **References:** `<362B44D8.24DB2691@bigfoot.com> <70h8c9$gl2$1@nnrp1.dejanews.com>`

```
I once had some time to kill while monitoring jobs, and wrote a passable
imitation of a CPU in DCL.  It would do arithmetic the way the CPU did,
with shift-lefts, etc.  It was quite inefficient, but wasn't limited to
what DCL could do.

If your application doesn't mind some efficiencies, you can do something
similar with COBOL.  You can treat each digit of a number separately
and  handle overflow the way you did doing arithmetic as a child.
```

#### ↳ Re: Calculating on 20 digits ammount?

- **From:** Einar Clementz <eclement@online.no>
- **Date:** 1998-10-25T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<36337C48.6C8ABB51@online.no>`
- **References:** `<362B44D8.24DB2691@bigfoot.com>`


### COBOL QUESTION

- **From:** skyraven@home.com (Sue L)

- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<362b65a5.5133357@news>`

```
Could someone please explain to me what an Operand error is? I have
looked through my text book that I'm working with at the college and
it's not explaining it to where it's actually making any sense. I'm
getting errors and it's driving me batty... someone care to take a
stab at this one?

Sue
```

- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol

- **From:** "Leif Svalgaard" <leif@ibm.net>
- **Date:** 1998-10-19T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<362b664d.0@news1.ibm.net>`

```

Sue L wrote in message <362b65a5.5133357@news>...
>Could someone please explain to me what an Operand error is? I have

>getting errors and it's driving me batty... someone care to take a

Sue,
always include the code or code snippet with the error.
From the meager information you supplied, I'll guess
that you have forgotten to place spaces on either side
of and operator (such as "+"), e.g.: compute a=b+1
is not valid, but compute a =  b  +  1 is
```

```
> looked through my text book that I'm working with at the college and
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
```

#### ↳ Re: COBOL QUESTION

- **From:** jyazel@freenet.columbus.oh.us (Jack Yazel)
- **Date:** 1998-10-19T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<70fvq6$bru@login.freenet.columbus.oh.us>`
- **References:** `<362b65a5.5133357@news>`

```
Sue L (skyraven@home.com) wrote:
: Could someone please explain to me what an Operand error is? I have
: looked through my text book that I'm working with at the college and
: it's not explaining it to where it's actually making any sense. I'm
: getting errors and it's driving me batty... someone care to take a
: stab at this one?
: Sue
   --------------------------

  The COBOL verbs (such as MOVE, ADD, MULTIPLY, etc.) generally
require additional information to complete successfully. The 
additional pieces of information are called operands.

  In addition, if you are making external calls, one or more
operands may be required.

  For instance, MOVE NAME TO LAST-NAME.

  MOVE is the verb.

  NAME is an operand and LAST-NAME is an operand.

  TO just makes the statement more readable.
```

#### ↳ Re: COBOL QUESTION

- **From:** "Donald Tees" <donald@willmack.com>
- **Date:** 1998-10-19T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<70g1ea$4ja$1@news.igs.net>`
- **References:** `<362b65a5.5133357@news>`

```

Sue L wrote in message <362b65a5.5133357@news>...
>Could someone please explain to me what an Operand error is? I have


Hard to tell without code, but I would guess that you have something
like "-1" in an arithmetic expression. When there is no space, the
compiler interprets it as a sign (as opposed to a "subtract"), thus there
is no operand.
```

#### ↳ Re: COBOL QUESTION

- **From:** "Dennis Gapper" <dgapper@bigpond.com>
- **Date:** 1998-10-22T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<362ef212.0@139.134.5.33>`
- **References:** `<362b65a5.5133357@news>`

```
Can u say if this is a compiler error message or a runtime error?  If the
latter, then the data in an operand field is at fault - very often as a
result of redefining fields as numeric/alphanumeric, and not being careful
with the contents.

Sue L wrote in message <362b65a5.5133357@news>...
>Could someone please explain to me what an Operand error is? I have
>looked through my text book that I'm working with at the college and
…[4 more quoted lines elided]…
>Sue
```

### Help Please With Fujitsu v3

- **From:** user@home.com (Grommit)
- **Date:** 1998-10-19T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<362b6e16.4873007@news.demon.co.uk>`

```
Does Any body have a copy of the serial to install this package, It
was supplied with  Cobol for Dummies which I have now misplaced.
Any Help Will be very welcome indeed.

John
```

#### ↳ Re: Help Please With Fujitsu v3

- **From:** "Judson McClendon" <judmc123@bellsouth.net>
- **Date:** 1998-10-19T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<qHLW1.95$xx4.189050@news3.mia.bellsouth.net>`
- **References:** `<362b6e16.4873007@news.demon.co.uk>`

```
Grommit wrote:
>Does Any body have a copy of the serial to install this package, It
>was supplied with  Cobol for Dummies which I have now misplaced.
>Any Help Will be very welcome indeed.

From Arthur Griffin, who wrote "COBOL for Dummies", posted here in
January:

--------------------------------------------------------------------
This serial number thing was an oversight. The installation
information for the other compilers is complete but, to install the
Fujitsu compiler, you may need to enter a serial number. If you do,
this one will work:
 103-2001 1699-03317-70168
The first part of the number should already be in the window during
the installation procedure, so all you will have to enter is
 99-03317-70168
The same number works for all the Fujitsu compilers.

Please accept my apologies for the omission but, hey, we all have the
right to be a dummy from time to time, right? If you have any other
problems, there is an 800 number at the bottom of page 368 that you
can use to find the answer to any other questions you might have about
the CD.
--------------------------------------------------------------------
```

### Re: Interested in Women's Y2K Mailing List?

- **From:** The Goobers <docdwarf@erols.com>

```
Shmuel (Seymour J.) Metz wrote:
> 
> Neason wrote:
…[5 more quoted lines elided]…
> not sure, ask your Grammar.

What for?... nobody listens to her, not even my Grampar.

But on a Programming Note... I remember, long ago, one of my instructors
putting up on the blackboard a record for a college-dormitory
student-tracking (no, not 'where are these kids?' but 'have these kids
PAID US MONEY!!!') system... and, in a fit of Spirit of the Times had a
provision for two students of differing genders sharing the same room. 
I spoke up, saying 'No, no, no, Mr Mullen; *nouns* have gender, *people*
have sex.'

The classroom giggled and, after a pause, Mr Mullen replied 'Well,
grammatically that might be the case but I will not follow those rules
for this record layout; think of what a depressing life it would be if
all you had to look forward to was SEX OCCURS 2 TIMES.'

DD



(headers expanded to include comp.lang.cobol)
```

<a id="t-4991861017"></a>
## Thread: Cobol Unleashed & Casegen — 1 msgs

### Cobol Unleashed & Casegen

- **From:** "Tim Allen" <tim@casegen.co.uk>
- **Date:** 1998-10-19T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<70ev94$9nj$3@newsreader2.core.theplanet.net>`

```
May we apologise to everyone who has tried using Casegen COBOL for Windows
supplied with the CD on "Cobol Unleashed" and had problems compiling their
programs.  You will have received this message:

'Registry error - cannot find compiler registry key... ".

This problem is due to an error with the Fujitsu compiler on the CD.  For
some reason the compiler does not get installed correctly and Casegen needs
this compiler to build Cobol programs generated by Casegen.

It will be necessary to install Fujitsu Version 3 or Version 4 from another
source to use Casegen.  We are currently discussing this matter with Fujitsu
to provide you with the correct software as soon as possible.

We hope to have a response in the next few days.

Again, our apologies - I know how frustating it can be trying to get
software to work, but I am sure you can appreciate that this problem is
outside our control.

If you need to try Casegen COBOL for Windows urgently, please call us on
+44 (0) 1494 451 014 during office hours (in the UK) and we will airmail you
a
CD.


Simon Cordingley
Development and Support Manager
Casegen Systems Ltd
```


#### ↳ Re: Debug help needed!!

- **From:** docdwarf@clark.net ()
- **Date:** 1998-10-19T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<70fifu$nhd$1@clarknet.clark.net>`
- **References:** `<362AA795.E831308E@bellsouth.net>`

```
In article <362AA795.E831308E@bellsouth.net>,
Vincent Lee  <vlee8@bellsouth.net> wrote:
>Could anyone have any suggestion on learning to read dumps? I don't seem
>to be able to find any information in any menu or popular books.

First one should learn to read Assembley.


DD
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Debug help needed!!

- **From:** Howard Brazee <NOSPAMbrazee@home.com>
- **Date:** 1998-10-19T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<362B69FC.3576676E@home.com>`
- **References:** `<362AA795.E831308E@bellsouth.net> <70fifu$nhd$1@clarknet.clark.net>`

```
It depends on what you are wanting to read.  In most COBOL shops the
standard is to put some strings in working storage such as:

01  FILLER   PIC X(40)  VALUE '***** WORKING STORAGE BEGINS HERE ****'.

some other fillers like this are nice before tables and other 01 levels. 

If you have these, you can look through your dump for your working
storage, and find out what's actually in your fields.  You have to
switch back and forth between text and hex (or octal), and know how to
translate binary and packed fields.  But the above technique will allow
you to find where to look.
```

#### ↳ Re: Debug help needed!!

- **From:** "Chris Osborne" <chris_n_osborne@yahoo.com>
- **Date:** 1998-10-19T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<362bba25.0@news3.uswest.net>`
- **References:** `<362AA795.E831308E@bellsouth.net>`

```

Vincent Lee wrote in message <362AA795.E831308E@bellsouth.net>...
>Could anyone have any suggestion on learning to read dumps? I don't seem
>to be able to find any information in any menu or popular books.
…[4 more quoted lines elided]…
>


COBOL II, by Harvey Bookman, has a section on understanding the Task Global
Table, but it doesn't have an explicit section on reading a dump.

If you're on an IBM platform, the manual VS COBOL II (IBM #SC26-4049-05),
contains a "Debugging Using Storage Dumps" section that is four chapters
long.
```

```
Position: Technical Support Manager

Experience: Responsible for support of production activities, ensuring
that hardware, applications and communications are optimally configured
for the use of outside customers.  Will manage all aspects of the AS/400
environment and the areas it supports.  Also will be responsible for
staffing, evaluations, and coordination of the computer operations
staff.  Must have a strong background in AS/400 operations in a
distribution environment, strong technical skills (COBOL, CLP,
Query/400, PC/LAN, TCP/IP, FTP, and IPX), and a proven record of
successful management abilities. BS in CS or equivalent degree is
mandatory along with at least 3 yrs. Of technical management experience.

Location: Western Burbs

Salary:   To $85,000

Contact: Jennifer Weisman
	Avalon Human Resources, Inc.
	Phone: 630-836-1255
	Fax: 630-836-1266
```

<a id="t-902e9163a1"></a>
## Thread: REALIA Cobol — 12 msgs

_Topics: [`Compilers and vendors`](topics.md#compilers)_

### REALIA Cobol

- **From:** "kennet" <kennet@post11.tele.dk>
- **Date:** 1998-10-20T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<70iokp$3ok4$1@news-inn.inet.tele.dk>`

```
Our support with Micro Focus has been cancelled, and we are looking for a
new COBOL workbench. Today we had a meeting at Computer Associates where
they showed us their product, REALIA (release 3). I don't recall seeing a
lot about this product (or earlier releases) in this newsgroup. Can anyone
tell me of their experience with this product (or with CA in general)? I'm
interested in knowing how it interacts with the mainframe and how well it
performs with pure PC programs, as we'll be needing both.

My impression was (from the demo) that the product looks a lot like Micro
Focus Workbench version 4.0.26 that we have been using thus far.

We are also considering other products, such as IBM (we have a meeting with
them thursday this week). Any comments on IBM's COBOL products?

Our current operating system on the workstations is OS/2, but probably soon
will be Windows NT (or Windows 98).

Thank you for any comment,
Kennet.
```

```
Quite so, now here's the other shoe. In general, the quality of a
product declines in direct proportion to how long CA has owned it. One
way of making money is to cut corners ruthlessly, firing all the good,
i.e., well paid, developers, and cutting support to the bone. Big bucks
for Charlie Wang, crappy products for the rest of us. They frequently
buy products with good (earned) reputations and coast on that for a
while.

Bill Lynch

abbreviation for cancer, among other things. Anything to it?
```

#### ↳ Re: REALIA Cobol

- **From:** redsky@ibm.net (Thane Hubbell)
- **Date:** 1998-10-20T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<Jl0PnHJ5PvPd-pn2-IVSRcvN3kdYs@Dwight_Miller.iix.com>`
- **References:** `<70iokp$3ok4$1@news-inn.inet.tele.dk>`

```
On Tue, 20 Oct 1998 19:35:00, "kennet" <kennet@post11.tele.dk> wrote:

> Our support with Micro Focus has been cancelled, and we are looking for a
> new COBOL workbench. Today we had a meeting at Computer Associates where
…[8 more quoted lines elided]…
> 

There is a lot that is different.  One thing is the limited support 
available if you are creating PC applications - says so right in the 
manual.

Next is the fact that the indexed file structure is very VSAM like.  
It does not allow record locking (at least with the NATIVE structure) 
and does not allow split keys.

Conversion from Realia to MF would be relatively easy - MF to Realia -
virtually impossible.

Realia has no 32 bit GUI - and thier 16 bit is a joke.  You could use 
a 3rd party product with Realia to create the GUI - something like 
COBOL SP2.

The other problem (and you will have this with any COBOL except maybe 
AcuCobol  - No 32 bit OS/2 support) (their 16 bit product - reportedly
dead - did have good OS/2 support).



> We are also considering other products, such as IBM (we have a meeting with
> them thursday this week). Any comments on IBM's COBOL products?
>

All reviews so far have been stellar - but I have no direct experience
- I think you will need a different version for each platform.  I 
don't know of anyone doing end user development with it - only in 
house and mainframe connectivity stuff.
 
> Our current operating system on the workstations is OS/2, but probably soon
> will be Windows NT (or Windows 98).
> 

Other vendors to consider are AcuCobol and Fujitsu.

Fujitsu has a really good 32 bit compiler for Windows 95/98/NT that 
has a good migration path from MicroFocus.

Question - why not move to a supported MicroFocus product?  Mad about 
them pulling the rug out from under you?  

The best PC COBOL solution I have seen for end user development is 
Fujitsu COBOL with COBOL Sp2 from flexus (Fujitsu: 
http://www.adtools.com, Flexus: http://www.flexus.com) SP2 for the GUI
and thier Form Print product for printing.  It all works and it works 
well.

Good luck.
```

- **Message-ID:** `<70j6gb$cnc@sjx-ixn3.ix.netcom.com>`

```

Thane Hubbell wrote in message ...
>On Tue, 20 Oct 1998 22:00:31, redsky@ibm.net (Thane Hubbell) wrote:
>
>> On Tue, 20 Oct 1998 19:35:00, "kennet" <kennet@post11.tele.dk> wrote:
>>
>> > Our support with Micro Focus has been cancelled, and we are looking for
a
>> > new COBOL workbench. Today we had a meeting at Computer Associates
where
>> > they showed us their product, REALIA (release 3). I don't recall seeing
a
>> > lot about this product (or earlier releases) in this newsgroup. Can
anyone
>> > tell me of their experience with this product (or with CA in general)?
I'm
>> > interested in knowing how it interacts with the mainframe and how well
it
>> > performs with pure PC programs, as we'll be needing both.
>> >
…[5 more quoted lines elided]…
>

Just so you don't get surprised, IBM VisualAge for COBOL *only* supports the
latest IBM mainframe dialects - no OS/VS COBOL (or DOS/VS COBOL) and no
CMPR2 support.  As both of those are supported by Micro Focus, you may want
to verify (before looking at IBM's workstation products) that this isn't an
issue for you.
```

#### ↳ Re: REALIA Cobol (positive)

- **From:** "NORCOM" <KHansen@SPAM_BLOCK_TEXTnorcomsoftware.com>
- **Date:** 1998-10-29T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<71b5ib$c7l$1@supernews.com>`
- **References:** `<70iokp$3ok4$1@news-inn.inet.tele.dk>`

```
We've been using CA-Realia COBOL for a long time (as well as the other guys'
products), and we like it.  The 32-bit workbench, like any of the workbench
products, takes a bit of getting used to, but it works well.  Not as easy
and efficient to use as the old DOS products, but given that everyone is
chasing after Windows and workbench suites, it's a nice product.

Our experience with support is sort of ho-hum, but that seems pretty
universal unless you pay big bucks for the premium support with any of the
vendors.

It's a fact that the PC environment takes considerably more know-how than
the mainframe environment (where the Technical Services guys hide the
low-level stuff from the application programmer).  We field lots of tech
support calls where it's obvious the programmer doesn't understand linking,
argument passing, and some of the more basic stuff about how a program goes
from source code to an executable application.  FWIW, it doesn't have much
to do with using our products, but we help 'em out all the same...

Although the manual says they don't support PC application development, it
is fine for that purpose.

We're using the CA-Realia COBOL Workbench 3.0 to develop a full GUI screen
manager to follow up on our popular ScreenIO product, and things are going
extremely well.
```

---

<a id="t-f825ead45e"></a>
## Thread: GVIM COBOL syntax hiliting columnar? — 2 msgs

### GVIM COBOL syntax hiliting columnar?

- **From:** borgnaes@freenet.columbus.oh.us (Chris Borgnaes)
- **Date:** 1998-10-20T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<70itf4$qq1@login.freenet.columbus.oh.us>`

```

Is there a way to make GVIM not look at columns 1-6 and 73-80 when COBOL
syntax hiliting is turned on?  It reds-out the entire line if there are
any comments in those areas.
```

#### ↳ Re: GVIM COBOL syntax hiliting columnar?

- **From:** cadams@cadams-ntw.acucorp.com (Chris Adams)
- **Date:** 1998-10-23T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<slrn731br0.1hc.cadams@cadams-ntw.acucorp.com>`
- **References:** `<70itf4$qq1@login.freenet.columbus.oh.us>`

```
On 20 Oct 1998 16:57:08 -0400, Chris Borgnaes <borgnaes@freenet.columbus.oh.us>
wrote:
>Is there a way to make GVIM not look at columns 1-6 and 73-80 when COBOL
>syntax hiliting is turned on?  It reds-out the entire line if there are any
>comments in those areas.

Disclaimer: I'm assuming that your gvim is at least somewhat similar to the vim
5.1 (1998 Apr 7) I'm using...

Somewhere you should have syntax directory with a number of .vim files,
including cobol.vim.  On my RedHat 5.1 linux box, this is
/usr/share/vim/syntax/ but YMMV.

Load cobol.vim in vim. There should be a block such as: 
-------------------------------------------------
if ! exists("cobol_legacy_code")
    " catch junk in columns 1-6 for modern code
    syn match cobolBAD      "^ \{0,5\}[^ ].*"
endif

" many legacy sources have junk in columns 1-6: must be before others
" Stuff after column 72 is in error - must be after all other "match" entries
if exists("cobol_legacy_code")
    syn match   cobolBadLine      "^.\{6}[^*/].\{66,\}"
else
    syn match   cobolBadLine      "^.\{6}.\{67,\}"
endif
-------------------------------------------------

That last syn match is the key: it's looking for 6 characters at the start of
the line followed by 67 characters, which is considered invalid since that ends
in column 73.

Here's the modified block I used to allow up to 120 character lines:
-------------------------------------------------
if ! exists("cobol_legacy_code")
    " catch junk in columns 1-6 for modern code
    syn match cobolBAD      "^ \{0,5\}[^ ].*"
endif

" many legacy sources have junk in columns 1-6: must be before others
" Stuff after column 72 is in error - must be after all other "match" entries
if exists("cobol_legacy_code")
    syn match   cobolBadLine      "^.\{6}[^*/].\{66,\}"
else
" CTA: Modified to allow anything out to column 120 for Acucobol terminal 
" format...
" Original:
"    syn match   cobolBadLine      "^.\{6}.\{67,\}"
    syn match   cobolBadLine      "^.\{6}.\{115,\}"

endif
-------------------------------------------------



If you need some help figuring out just what to do, check out the online help
":h syntax".
```

---

<a id="t-978c9794e4"></a>
## Thread: Lower Case To Upper Case in COBOLII — 24 msgs

_Topics: [`Compilers and vendors`](topics.md#compilers)_

### Lower Case To Upper Case in COBOLII

- **From:** akent@seanet.com (Arthur M. Kent)
- **Date:** 1998-10-20T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<akent-2010981903230001@u29.dialup.seanet.com>`

```
I have, I believe, a rather simple question that (hopefully) someone can
help me with.  We have an application in which the names of customers were
"incorrectly" entered with mixed case, ie, John Smith.  In order to do an
online search of this application by customer name, however, the name must
be in all caps, ie, JOHN SMITH.

I am using COBOL II on an IBM Mainframe, under MVS/JCL.  I looked in some
COBOL manuals earlier today, but I did not see anything which indicates
how one processes "mixed case" for an Alpha field.

I would appreciate some assistance as to how one can "convert" lower case
to upper case using COBOL II.

Thanks,
Arthur M. Kent
```

#### ↳ Re: Lower Case To Upper Case in COBOLII

- **From:** "M.Kobobel" <classicmek@sprynet.com>
- **Date:** 1998-10-20T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<70js7i$4rd$1@juliana.sprynet.com>`
- **References:** `<akent-2010981903230001@u29.dialup.seanet.com>`

```
<<I would appreciate some assistance as to how one can
"convert" lower case
to upper case using COBOL II.>>

Long, Long...... time ago, in the days of COBOL 68 I
occasionally had to do a conversion because the operator was
forever entering lower case (or partial lower case answers)
into the console.

It took a character by character evaluation of the subject
field. First, you move low-values to a pic 9(4) comp field.
Then you move the individual character to the last position
of a pic xx field (that redefines the pic 9(4) binary
field).  Then, check  the binary field for greater than 193
(if it is, it doesn't need converting).  If it isn't add 64
to the binary field, and move the second character of the
pic xx field back to the original location.  This assumes
you're using EBCDIC and the field doesn't contain
punctuation or spaces.  If the field can contain any
punctuation or spaces, you'll want to check for those values
and not convert them either.
```

#### ↳ Re: Lower Case To Upper Case in COBOLII

- **From:** redsky@ibm.net (Thane Hubbell)
- **Date:** 1998-10-21T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<Jl0PnHJ5PvPd-pn2-4d6CSzkxhCeb@Dwight_Miller.iix.com>`
- **References:** `<akent-2010981903230001@u29.dialup.seanet.com>`

```
On Wed, 21 Oct 1998 02:03:22, akent@seanet.com (Arthur M. Kent) wrote:

> I would appreciate some assistance as to how one can "convert" lower case
> to upper case using COBOL II.
>


If it is TRULY COBOL II - and Not "COBOL for this and that", this is 
your best solution:

Inspect Input-Field Converting "abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz" 
                                                    TO 
"ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ"

However, if you have the later cobol, use

Move Function Upper-Case (Input-Field) to Input-Field 

(Yes you CAN do this - but I prefer a different destination field).

You can LEAVE the data in mixed case in the file, and just convert to 
upper for the search.
```

#### ↳ Re: Lower Case To Upper Case in COBOLII

- **From:** Howard Brazee <NOSPAMbrazee@home.com>
- **Date:** 1998-10-21T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<362E1B68.8CB7626D@home.com>`
- **References:** `<akent-2010981903230001@u29.dialup.seanet.com>`

```
> I would appreciate some assistance as to how one can "convert" lower case
> to upper case using COBOL II.

Try INSPECT. Other COBOLs could use TRANSFORM or EXAMINE.

My COBOL II installation does not have INSPECT CONVERTING or functions,
so I have to do the following.

 INSPECT HJBDEBUG, REPLACING  ALL 'a' by 'a'
                                  'b' by 'B'  etc, etc.
 
I once worked in a shop which had a mixture of ANSI 68 and ANSI 72
COBOL.  It used EXAMINE to do the job.  Since EXAMINE was replaced by
INSPECT, I decided to use the simpler command which was available in
both ANSI versions:

TRANSFORM TEST-NAME FROM
  'abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz' TO
  'ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ'.

I thought this would be safe.  Well, now TRANSFORM is obsolete.  So
existing, working code has been changed twice to fit new ANSI standards.

Too bad, if I am between TRANSFORM and INSPECT CONVERTING, I have to
have clumsy code.
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Lower Case To Upper Case in COBOLII

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 1998-10-22T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<70o7ed$r8f@sjx-ixn4.ix.netcom.com>`
- **References:** `<akent-2010981903230001@u29.dialup.seanet.com> <362E1B68.8CB7626D@home.com>`

```

Howard Brazee wrote in message <362E1B68.8CB7626D@home.com>...
   <snip>
>
>My COBOL II installation does not have INSPECT CONVERTING or functions,
>so I have to do the following.
>
  <more snip>

I believe (know) that your VS COBOL II compiler doesn't have the intrinsic
functions - but I suggest that you check again.  I know that all versions of
VS COBOL II from release 3.0 up *do* have INSPECT CONVERTING - and I am
actually pretty certain that some of the earlier ones do too - but as they
haven't been supported for over a decade, I am not positive.
```

#### ↳ Re: Lower Case To Upper Case in COBOLII

- **From:** sff5ky@aol.com (Sff5ky)
- **Date:** 1998-10-21T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<19981020231024.23759.00001643@ngol03.aol.com>`
- **References:** `<akent-2010981903230001@u29.dialup.seanet.com>`

```

In article <akent-2010981903230001@u29.dialup.seanet.com>, akent@seanet.com
(Arthur M. Kent) writes:

> would appreciate some assistance as to how one can "convert" lower case
>to upper case using COBOL II.
>

Check on the syntax usage of FUNCTION UPPER-CASE(field-name)
i.e. 
    IF FUNCTION UPPER-CASE(field-name) = 'SOME STRING'
        do true condtion
```
